NameFlagsCard.TypeDescription & Constraints
DiagnosticReport 0..*QICoreDiagnosticReportLabA Diagnostic report - a combination of request information, atomic results, images, interpretation, as well as formatted reports
meta 0..1Meta314eMetadata about the resource
Slices for extension 0..*ExtensionExtension
Slice: Unordered, Open by value:url
extension:additionalInfo 0..*Reference314e()Supplementary information for this resource
URL: http://314e.com/fhir/StructureDefinition/resource-additionalInfo
identifier 0..*Identifier314eBusiness identifier for report
basedOn 0..*Reference314e(314e CarePlan | 314e ImmunizationRecommendation | 314e MedicationRequest | 314e NutritionOrder | 314e ServiceRequest)(QI) What was requested
category 1..*CodeableConcept314e(QI) Service category
Slices for category Content/Rules for all slices
category:LaboratorySlice 1..1CodeableConcept314e(QI) Service category
category:subCategory 0..1CodeableConcept314eOptional detailed subcategory
Binding: 314e Procedure Subcategories (required)
code 1..1CodeableConcept314e(QI) US Core Laboratory Report Order Code
subject 1..1Reference314e(314e Patient)(QI) The subject of the report - usually, but not always, the patient
encounter 0..1Reference314e(314e Encounter)(QI) Encounter associated with DiagnosticReport
effective[x] 0..1(QI) Diagnostically relevant time (typically the time of specimen collection)
effectiveDateTimedateTime(dateTime314e)
effectivePeriodPeriod(Period314e)
issued 0..1instant314e(QI) DateTime this version was made
performer 0..*Reference314e(314e Practitioner | 314e PractitionerRole | 314e Organization)(QI) Responsible Diagnostic Service
resultsInterpreter 0..*Reference314e(314e Practitioner | 314e PractitionerRole | 314e Organization | 314e CareTeam)Primary result interpreter
specimen 0..*Reference314e(314e Specimen)Specimens this report is based on
result 0..*Reference314e(314e General Laboratory Observation | 314e Microbiology Observation | 314e Microorganism Observation | 314e Antimicrobial Susceptibility Observation)(QI) Observations

media

Slices for presentedForm 0..*AttachmentEntire report as issued
Slice: Unordered, Open by value:title

presentedForm:notes 0..*Attachment314eAnnotation/ notes associated with the diagnostic report
title 1..1stringLabel to display in place of the data
Required Pattern: Note

Documentation for this format

name: DiagnosticReportLab314e
title: 314e DiagnosticReport Laboratory Results
description:
314e-constrained DiagnosticReport profile for laboratory results reporting,
derived from QI-Core DiagnosticReport Profile for Laboratory Results Reporting.

This profile applies 314e-defined extensions and uses 314e datatype profiles
where applicable.

element.short: A Diagnostic report - a combination of request information, atomic results, images, interpretation, as well as formatted reports
element.definition: The findings and interpretation of diagnostic  tests performed on patients, groups of patients, devices, and locations, and/or specimens derived from these. The report includes clinical context such as requesting and provider information, and some mix of atomic results, images, textual and coded interpretations, and formatted representation of diagnostic reports.
element.comment: This is intended to capture a single report and is not suitable for use in displaying summary information that covers multiple reports.  For example, this resource has not been designed for laboratory cumulative reporting formats nor detailed structured reports for sequencing.

constraint.human: If the resource is contained in another resource, it SHALL NOT contain nested Resources
If the resource is contained in another resource, it SHALL be referred to from elsewhere in the resource or SHALL refer to the containing resource
constraint.expression: contained.where((('#'+id in (%resource.descendants().reference | %resource.descendants().as(canonical) | %resource.descendants().as(uri) | %resource.descendants().as(url))) or descendants().where(reference = '#').exists() or descendants().where(as(canonical) = '#').exists() or descendants().where(as(canonical) = '#').exists()).not()).trace('unmatched', id).empty()
constraint.xpath: not(exists(for $id in f:contained/*/f:id/@value return $contained[not(parent::*/descendant::f:reference/@value=concat('#', $contained/*/id/@value) or descendant::f:reference[@value='#'])]))

constraint.human: If a resource is contained in another resource, it SHALL NOT have a meta.versionId or a meta.lastUpdated
If a resource is contained in another resource, it SHALL NOT have a security label

extension.valueMarkdown: When a resource has no narrative, only systems that fully understand the data can display the resource to a human safely. Including a human readable representation in the resource makes for a much more robust eco-system and cheaper handling of resources by intermediary systems. Some ecosystems restrict distribution of resources to only those systems that do fully understand the resources, and as a consequence implementers may believe that the narrative is superfluous. However experience shows that such eco-systems often open up to new participants over time.

constraint.human: A resource should have narrative for robust management
effective[x] SHALL be present if the status is 'partial', 'preliminary', 'final', 'amended', 'corrected' or 'appended'
constraint.expression: (status='partial' or status='preliminary' or status='final' or status='amended' or status='corrected' or status='appended' ) implies effective.exists()

constraint.human: issued SHALL be present if the status is 'partial', 'preliminary', 'final', 'amended', 'corrected' or 'appended'
constraint.expression: (status='partial' or status='preliminary' or status='final' or status='amended' or status='corrected' or status='appended' ) implies issued.exists()

element.definition: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.
element.comment: The only time that a resource does not have an id is when it is being submitted to the server using a create operation.

element.definition: The metadata about the resource. This is content that is maintained by the infrastructure. Changes to the content might not always be associated with version changes to the resource.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.definition: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

slicing.description: Extensions are always sliced by (at least) url

element.definition: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.
element.comment: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions.  The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.definition: The version specific identifier, as it appears in the version portion of the URL. This value changes when the resource is created, updated, or deleted.
element.comment: The server assigns this value, and ignores what the client specifies, except in the case that the server is imposing version integrity on updates/deletes.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.definition: When the resource last changed - e.g. when the version changed.
element.comment: This value is always populated except when the resource is first being created. The server / resource manager sets this value; what a client provides is irrelevant. This is equivalent to the HTTP Last-Modified and SHOULD have the same value on a [read](http://hl7.org/fhir/R4/http.html#read) interaction.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Identifies where the resource comes from
element.definition: A uri that identifies the source system of the resource. This provides a minimal amount of [Provenance](http://hl7.org/fhir/R4/provenance.html#) information that can be used to track or differentiate the source of information in the resource. The source may identify another FHIR server, document, message, database, etc.
element.comment:
In the provenance resource, this corresponds to Provenance.entity.what[x]. The exact use of the source (and the implied Provenance.entity.role) is left to implementer discretion. Only one nominated source is allowed; for additional provenance details, a full Provenance resource should be used. 

This element can be used to indicate where the current master source of a resource that has a canonical URL if the resource is no longer hosted at the canonical URL.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Profiles this resource claims to conform to
element.definition: A list of profiles (references to [StructureDefinition](http://hl7.org/fhir/R4/structuredefinition.html#) resources) that this resource claims to conform to. The URL is a reference to [StructureDefinition.url](http://hl7.org/fhir/R4/structuredefinition-definitions.html#StructureDefinition.url).
element.comment: It is up to the server and/or other infrastructure of policy to determine whether/how these claims are verified and/or updated over time.  The list of profile URLs is a set.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Security Labels applied to this resource
element.definition: Security labels applied to this resource. These tags connect specific resources to the overall security policy and infrastructure.
element.comment: The security labels can be updated without changing the stated version of the resource. The list of security labels is a set. Uniqueness is based the system/code, and version and display are ignored.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

binding.description: Security Labels from the Healthcare Privacy and Security Classification System.

element.definition: Tags applied to this resource. Tags are intended to be used to identify and relate resources to process and workflow, and applications are not required to consider the tags when interpreting the meaning of a resource.
element.comment: The tags can be updated without changing the stated version of the resource. The list of tags is a set. Uniqueness is based the system/code, and version and display are ignored.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

binding.description: Codes that represent various types of tags, commonly workflow-related; e.g. "Needs review by Dr. Jones".

element.short: A set of rules under which this content was created
element.definition: A reference to a set of rules that were followed when the resource was constructed, and which must be understood when processing the content. Often, this is a reference to an implementation guide that defines the special rules along with other profiles etc.
element.comment: Asserting this rule set restricts the content to be only understood by a limited set of trading partners. This inherently limits the usefulness of the data in the long term. However, the existing health eco-system is highly fractured, and not yet ready to define, collect, and exchange data in a generally computable sense. Wherever possible, implementers and/or specification writers should avoid using this element. Often, when used, the URL is a reference to an implementation guide that defines these special rules as part of it's narrative along with other profiles, value sets, etc.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.isModifierReason: This element is labeled as a modifier because the implicit rules may provide additional knowledge about the resource that modifies it's meaning or interpretation

element.definition: The base language in which the resource is written.
element.comment: Language is provided to support indexing and accessibility (typically, services such as text to speech use the language tag). The html language tag in the narrative applies  to the narrative. The language tag on the resource may be used to specify the language of other presentations generated from the data in the resource. Not all the content has to be in the base language. The Resource.language should not be assumed to apply to the narrative automatically. If a language is specified, it should it also be specified on the div element in the html (see rules in HTML5 for information about the relationship between xml:lang and the html lang attribute).

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Text summary of the resource, for human interpretation
element.definition: A human-readable narrative that contains a summary of the resource and can be used to represent the content of the resource to a human. The narrative need not encode all the structured data, but is required to contain sufficient detail to make it "clinically safe" for a human to just read the narrative. Resource definitions may define what content should be represented in the narrative to ensure clinical safety.
element.comment: Contained resources do not have narrative. Resources that are not contained SHOULD have a narrative. In some cases, a resource may only have text with little or no additional discrete data (as long as all minOccurs=1 elements are satisfied).  This may be necessary for data from legacy systems where information is captured as a "text blob" or where text is additionally entered raw or narrated and encoded information is added later.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.definition: These resources do not have an independent existence apart from the resource that contains them - they cannot be identified independently, and nor can they have their own independent transaction scope.
element.comment: This should never be done when the content can be identified properly, as once identification is lost, it is extremely difficult (and context dependent) to restore it again. Contained resources may have profiles and tags In their meta elements, but SHALL NOT have security labels.

element.definition: An Extension

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.definition:
Applied at the root level of any resource. Used to capture supplementary
or non-standard information related to a FHIR resource that is not
represented by the core FHIR elements or existing profiles.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.definition:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the resource and that modifies the understanding of the element that contains it and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer is allowed to define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.

Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).
element.comment: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions.  The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.
element.requirements: Modifier extensions allow for extensions that *cannot* be safely ignored to be clearly distinguished from the vast majority of extensions which can be safely ignored.  This promotes interoperability by eliminating the need for implementers to prohibit the presence of extensions. For further information, see the [definition of modifier extensions](http://hl7.org/fhir/R4/extensibility.html#modifierExtension).

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.isModifierReason: Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the resource that contains them

element.definition: Identifiers assigned to this report by the performer or other systems.
element.comment: Usually assigned by the Information System of the diagnostic service provider (filler id).
element.requirements: Need to know what identifier to use when making queries about this report from the source laboratory, and for linking to the report outside FHIR context.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

mapping.map: OBR-51/ for globally unique filler ID  - OBR-3 , For non-globally unique filler-id the flller/placer number must be combined with the universal service Id -  OBR-2(if present)+OBR-3+OBR-4

element.definition: Details concerning a service requested.
element.comment: Note: Usually there is one test request for each result, however in some circumstances multiple test requests may be represented using a single test result resource. Note that there are also cases where one request leads to multiple reports.
element.requirements: This allows tracing of authorization for the report and tracking whether proposals/recommendations were acted upon.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.definition: The status of the diagnostic report.
element.requirements: Diagnostic services routinely issue provisional/incomplete reports, and sometimes withdraw previously released reports.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.isModifierReason: This element is labeled as a modifier because it is a status element that contains status entered-in-error which means that the resource should not be treated as valid

mapping.map: statusCode  Note: final and amended are distinguished by whether observation is the subject of a ControlAct event of type "revise"

element.definition: A code that classifies the clinical discipline, department or diagnostic service that created the report (e.g. cardiology, biochemistry, hematology, MRI). This is used for searching, sorting and display purposes.
element.comment: Multiple categories are allowed using various categorization schemes.   The level of granularity is defined by the category concepts in the value set. More fine-grained filtering can be performed using the metadata and/or terminology hierarchy in DiagnosticReport.code.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.definition: A code that classifies the clinical discipline, department or diagnostic service that created the report (e.g. cardiology, biochemistry, hematology, MRI). This is used for searching, sorting and display purposes.
element.comment: Multiple categories are allowed using various categorization schemes.   The level of granularity is defined by the category concepts in the value set. More fine-grained filtering can be performed using the metadata and/or terminology hierarchy in DiagnosticReport.code.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.definition:
More granular operational sub-classification of the
diagnostic report.
element.comment: Multiple categories are allowed using various categorization schemes.   The level of granularity is defined by the category concepts in the value set. More fine-grained filtering can be performed using the metadata and/or terminology hierarchy in DiagnosticReport.code.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: (QI) US Core Laboratory Report Order Code
element.definition: The test, panel or battery that was ordered.
element.comment: UsageNote= The typical patterns for codes are:  1)  a LOINC code either as a  translation from a "local" code or as a primary code, or 2)  a local code only if no suitable LOINC exists,  or 3)  both the local and the LOINC translation.   Systems SHALL be capable of sending the local code if one exists.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

mapping.map: OBR-4 (HL7 v2 doesn't provide an easy way to indicate both the ordered test and the performed panel)

element.short: (QI) The subject of the report - usually, but not always, the patient
element.definition: The subject of the report. Usually, but not always, this is a patient. However, diagnostic services also perform analyses on specimens collected from a variety of other sources.
element.requirements: SHALL know the subject context.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

mapping.map: PID-3 (no HL7 v2 mapping for Group or Device)

element.definition: The healthcare event  (e.g. a patient and healthcare provider interaction) which this DiagnosticReport is about.
element.comment: This will typically be the encounter the event occurred within, but some events may be initiated prior to or after the official completion of an encounter  but still be tied to the context of the encounter  (e.g. pre-admission laboratory tests).
element.requirements: Links the request to the Encounter context.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: (QI) Diagnostically relevant time (typically the time of specimen collection)
element.definition: The time or time-period the observed values are related to. When the subject of the report is a patient, this is usually either the time of the procedure or of specimen collection(s), but very often the source of the date/time is not known, only the date/time itself.
element.comment: If the diagnostic procedure was performed on the patient, this is the time it was performed. If there are specimens, the diagnostically relevant time can be derived from the specimen collection times, but the specimen information is not always available, and the exact relationship between the specimens and the diagnostically relevant time is not always automatic.
element.requirements: Need to know where in the patient history to file/present this report.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.definition: The date and time that this version of the report was made available to providers, typically after the report was reviewed and verified.
element.comment: May be different from the update time of the resource itself, because that is the status of the record (potentially a secondary copy), not the actual release time of the report.
element.requirements: Clinicians need to be able to check the date that the report was released.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.definition: The diagnostic service that is responsible for issuing the report.
element.comment: This is not necessarily the source of the atomic data items or the entity that interpreted the results. It is the entity that takes responsibility for the clinical report.
element.requirements: Need to know whom to contact if there are queries about the results. Also may need to track the source of reports for secondary data analysis.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.definition: The practitioner or organization that is responsible for the report's conclusions and interpretations.
element.comment: Might not be the same entity that takes responsibility for the clinical report.
element.requirements: Need to know whom to contact if there are queries about the results. Also may need to track the source of reports for secondary data analysis.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Specimens this report is based on
element.definition: Details about the specimens on which this diagnostic report is based.
element.comment: If the specimen is sufficiently specified with a code in the test result name, then this additional data may be redundant. If there are multiple specimens, these may be represented per observation or group.
element.requirements: Need to be able to report information about the collected specimens on which the report is based.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.definition: [Observations](http://hl7.org/fhir/R4/observation.html)  that are part of this diagnostic report.
element.comment: Observations can contain observations.
element.requirements: Need to support individual results, or  groups of results, where the result grouping is arbitrary, but meaningful.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Reference to full details of imaging associated with the diagnostic report
element.definition: One or more links to full details of any imaging performed during the diagnostic investigation. Typically, this is imaging performed by DICOM enabled modalities, but this is not required. A fully enabled PACS viewer can use this information to provide views of the source images.
element.comment: ImagingStudy and the image element are somewhat overlapping - typically, the list of image references in the image element will also be found in one of the imaging study resources. However, each caters to different types of displays for different types of purposes. Neither, either, or both may be provided.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Key images associated with this report
element.definition: A list of key images associated with this report. The images are generally created during the diagnostic process, and may be directly of the patient, or of treated specimens (i.e. slides of interest).
element.requirements: Many diagnostic services include images in the report as part of their service.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.definition: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.
element.comment: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions.  The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.short: Extensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized
element.definition:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.

Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).
element.comment: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions.  The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.
element.requirements: Modifier extensions allow for extensions that *cannot* be safely ignored to be clearly distinguished from the vast majority of extensions which can be safely ignored.  This promotes interoperability by eliminating the need for implementers to prohibit the presence of extensions. For further information, see the [definition of modifier extensions](http://hl7.org/fhir/R4/extensibility.html#modifierExtension).

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.isModifierReason: Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them

element.definition: A comment about the image. Typically, this is used to provide an explanation for why the image is included, or to draw the viewer's attention to important features.
element.comment: The comment should be displayed with the image. It would be common for the report to include additional discussion of the image contents in other sections such as the conclusion.
element.requirements: The provider of the report should make a comment about each image included in the report.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.definition: Reference to the image source.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.definition: Concise and clinically contextualized summary conclusion (interpretation/impression) of the diagnostic report.
element.requirements: Need to be able to provide a conclusion that is not lost among the basic result data.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Codes for the clinical conclusion of test results
element.definition: One or more codes that represent the summary conclusion (interpretation/impression) of the diagnostic report.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

binding.description: Diagnosis codes provided as adjuncts to the report.

element.definition: Rich text representation of the entire result as issued by the diagnostic service. Multiple formats are allowed but they SHALL be semantically equivalent.
element.comment: "application/pdf" is recommended as the most reliable and interoperable in this context.
element.requirements: Gives laboratory the ability to provide its own fully formatted report for clinical fidelity.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.definition: Rich text representation of the entire result as issued by the diagnostic service. Multiple formats are allowed but they SHALL be semantically equivalent.
element.comment: "application/pdf" is recommended as the most reliable and interoperable in this context.
element.requirements: Gives laboratory the ability to provide its own fully formatted report for clinical fidelity.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Annotation/ notes associated with the diagnostic report
element.definition: Rich text representation of the entire result as issued by the diagnostic service. Multiple formats are allowed but they SHALL be semantically equivalent.
element.comment: "application/pdf" is recommended as the most reliable and interoperable in this context.
element.requirements: Gives laboratory the ability to provide its own fully formatted report for clinical fidelity.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.definition: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

slicing.description: Extensions are always sliced by (at least) url

element.definition: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.
element.comment: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions.  The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.short: Semantic categorization tag associated with the attachment
element.definition:
A categorization or semantic tag associated with an Attachment.

This extension allows multiple tags to be associated with an
Attachment for workflow, classification, indexing, retrieval,
processing, or application behavior purposes.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.short: Helper file associated with the attachment
element.definition:
Provides the path or filename of an auxiliary/helper file required
for interpretation, transformation, rendering, or processing of
the attachment content.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.short: Mime type of the content, with charset etc.
element.definition: Identifies the type of the data in the attachment and allows a method to be chosen to interpret or render the data. Includes mime type parameters such as charset where appropriate.
element.requirements: Processors of the data need to be able to know how to interpret the data.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

binding.description: The mime type of an attachment. Any valid mime type is allowed.

mapping.map: ED.2+ED.3/RP.2+RP.3. Note conversion may be needed if old style values are being used

element.definition: The human language of the content. The value can be any valid value according to BCP 47.
element.requirements: Users need to be able to choose between the languages in a set of attachments.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.definition:
Inline binary data SHALL NOT be populated.

Attachment content SHALL instead be persisted externally
and referenced using Attachment.url.
element.comment: The base64-encoded data SHALL be expressed in the same character set as the base resource XML or JSON.
element.requirements: The data needs to able to be transmitted inline.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Object storage path or external attachment location
element.definition:
Reference/path to the externally stored attachment content. Inline binary data in 
Attachment.data SHALL NOT be populated. Inline content SHALL instead be persisted 
externally, referenced using this url element, and tagged as 'inline-data-externalized-to-file'.
element.comment: If both data and url are provided, the url SHALL point to the same content as the data contains. Urls may be relative references or may reference transient locations such as a wrapping envelope using cid: though this has ramifications for using signatures. Relative URLs are interpreted relative to the service url, like a resource reference, rather than relative to the resource itself. If a URL is provided, it SHALL resolve to actual data.
element.requirements: The data needs to be transmitted by reference.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

mapping.map: RP.1+RP.2 - if they refer to a URL (see v2.6)

element.short: Number of bytes of content (if url provided)
element.definition: The number of bytes of data that make up this attachment (before base64 encoding, if that is done).
element.comment: The number of bytes is redundant if the data is provided as a base64binary, but is useful if the data is provided as a url reference.
element.requirements: Representing the size allows applications to determine whether they should fetch the content automatically in advance, or refuse to fetch it at all.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.definition: The calculated hash of the data using SHA-1. Represented using base64.
element.comment: The hash is calculated on the data prior to base64 encoding, if the data is based64 encoded. The hash is not intended to support digital signatures. Where protection against malicious threats a digital signature should be considered, see [Provenance.signature](http://hl7.org/fhir/R4/provenance-definitions.html#Provenance.signature) for mechanism to protect a resource with a digital signature.
element.requirements: Included so that applications can verify that the contents of a location have not changed due to technical failures (e.g., storage rot, transport glitch, incorrect version).

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Label to display in place of the data
element.definition: A label or set of text to display in place of the data.
element.requirements: Applications need a label to display to a human user in place of the actual data if the data cannot be rendered or perceived by the viewer.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.definition: The date that the attachment was first created.
element.requirements: This is often tracked as an integrity issue for use of the attachment.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.definition:
More granular operational sub-classification of the
diagnostic report.

element.short: Annotation/ notes associated with the diagnostic report
